free_text: Cross sections for the production of Pa, Th, and Ac
isotopes in the decays of highly excited compound
nuclei 221,223,225,227-Pa formed in reactions featuring
20,22-Ne and 24,26-Mg ions
In Russian
Transl. an English
The energy of the bombarding ions that have
traversed the absorbers was determined by the energy of
ions scattered by a thin gold foil (250 mug/cm2 in
thickness) at an angle of 30 degree.
Corrections for nonionization losses and for energy
losses in the "dead" layer of the detector were not
taken into account.
The detection of recoil nuclei and the
measurement of the energies of their alpha decays were
performed in the focal plane of the separator by a
recording system formed by two wide-aperture
time-of-flight detectors with a time resolution of
0.5 ns and an eight-strip semiconductor detector having
the total dimensions of 50 X 70 mm2 and a resolution of
30 keV for alpha particles with energies in the range
6-9 MeV.
The absolute values of bombarding-ion energies
to within 1.5% (2.0 MeV). The accuracy in determining
the relative values of ion energies is 2 to 3 times
higher, amounting to +/-(0.7-1.0) MeV.
Total uncertainty of cross section values.
It includes uncertainties of yields, separation
efficiency, target thickness and the flux of ions
The accuracy in measuring the yields of
individual isotopes
Rotating targets made of the separated
isotope 205-Tl enriched to (99.6 +/- 0.1)% and the
197-Au isotope with mean thicknesses of 350+/-50 and
420+/-50 ug/cm**2, respectively. The targets were
manufactured by sputtering the above isotopes onto
aluminum foils 6 mum thick.  The method of X-ray-
fluorescent analysis was used to measure the
thicknesses of the targets and their homogeneity.
Remarks of VML are included.
Last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
